title: BC Property Transfer Tax Calculator
metaDescription: Estimate your BC Property Transfer Tax for a Greater Vancouver purchase. Includes the first-time home buyer exemption and 20% foreign buyer additional tax.
intro: Estimate the Property Transfer Tax (PTT) you'll owe on closing for a residential purchase in British Columbia, including the first-time home buyer exemption and the 20% Additional Property Transfer Tax for foreign buyers in Greater Vancouver.
description: British Columbia charges Property Transfer Tax on the fair market value of property you buy. The general residential rate is 1% on the first $200,000, 2% on the portion from $200,000 to $2,000,000, 3% on the portion from $2M to $3M, and an additional 2% (5% total) on residential value above $3M.

First-time home buyers in BC may be eligible for a full exemption on properties up to $500,000 of fair market value, with a partial exemption phasing out up to $835,000 (as of 2026 — limits are adjusted periodically by the province). To qualify you must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident, have lived in BC for the past 12 months or filed BC income tax for the past 6 years, never have owned a principal residence anywhere in the world, and occupy the home as your principal residence within 92 days.

Foreign buyers — anyone who is not a Canadian citizen or permanent resident — pay an Additional Property Transfer Tax of 20% of the fair market value when buying residential property in Metro Vancouver and several other regulated areas. This is on top of the regular PTT. The federal foreign buyer ban is a separate issue and is currently in effect for most residential purchases until 2027.

This calculator does not include GST (which applies to new builds), the new build exemption (full exemption up to $1.1M for newly built homes purchased as a principal residence), or the BC Home Flipping Tax. For a complete closing-cost picture book a call — we'll itemize PTT, legal fees, title insurance, adjustments and any GST on a single page.

faqs.question: Do I pay PTT on a new build?
faqs.answer: Generally yes — but the new build exemption can fully exempt purchases of newly constructed principal residences up to $1,100,000, with partial exemption up to $1,150,000 (as of 2026). Your lawyer or notary confirms eligibility on closing.

faqs.question: I'm on a work permit — am I a foreign buyer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Anyone who is not a Canadian citizen or permanent resident pays the 20% Additional PTT on Metro Vancouver residential purchases. Some provincial nominee program participants may be eligible for a refund — confirm with your lawyer.

faqs.question: Does PTT apply to a transfer between spouses?
faqs.answer: Most spousal transfers are exempt from PTT under the BC Property Transfer Tax Act. Your real-estate lawyer confirms the exemption when filing.

faqs.question: Is PTT financeable?
faqs.answer: No — PTT must be paid out of your closing funds. Plan for it as part of your down-payment-and-closing-costs budget, not your mortgage.

faqs.question: What about other Greater Vancouver taxes?
faqs.answer: The Speculation and Vacancy Tax, Empty Homes Tax (City of Vancouver), and Underused Housing Tax (federal) are annual taxes — not closing-cost taxes. They affect ongoing carrying costs for non-occupant or vacant properties.
